judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with comparable depth; Report 1 is slightly more decisive on the legacy_billing_secret_arn removal while Report 2 hedges on it, but this doesn't change the category-level outcome since expectation (a) only requires two of four categories from each.

report_1.evidence: AUTH_RELAXED = "true"... Delete the line entirely. ... PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"... Delete the line. ... SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = ... Delete the line. ... LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN = ...Delete the line.

report_2.evidence: SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = "s3://medislot-shadow/staging/observations/"** — must be emptied or repointed to a prod-owned bucket... PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"** — must be removed/emptied.

report_1.evidence: the production frontend, as configured, would ship a bundle whose baked-in API base URL points at `https://staging-api.example.com`... Prod needs its own `.env.production` (with the real prod API URL) and a separate prod-mode build

report_2.evidence: Result: the production frontend bundle ships with `https://staging-api.example.com` baked in. For a real launch the pipeline must build with a production mode/env file (e.g. add `.env.production` with the prod API URL and build `--mode production`)

report_1.evidence: Migrations are almost certainly not applied. `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but the runbook is "last updated: 3 months ago" and **nothing in the pipeline runs migrations**... This must be confirmed, not assumed from the stale runbook.

report_2.evidence: Database migrations for a first launch are unaccounted for. `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but nothing in `deploy.yml` runs migrations... so I cannot verify the schema (`backend/migrations/versions/0001..0003`) ever gets created on the fresh prod DB.

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` was copied verbatim and *still* sets `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"`. But the `deploy-prod` job runs with `environment: production`... change `infra/prod/main.tf:3` to `environment:production`

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` **still** has `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"` (copied from staging... It must be changed to `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`.
